title: TC001-Search doctors by specialization and view matching results
description: Verifies a logged-in patient can search by specialization and see a list of matching doctors.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the login page at /login (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:5173/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//h2[text()="Search results"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Search doctors by doctor name and open doctor details
description: Verifies a logged-in patient can search by a doctor's name, open a doctor card, and see profile/availability information.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click 'Accept & Continue' (index 52) to close consent, then fill Email (index 63) and Password (index 71) with provided credentials, then click 'Sign In' (index 82) to attempt login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('abhishekedla9133@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('8309176874')
        
        # -> Fill the visible email and password inputs (indexes 185 and 193) with the provided credentials and click the Sign In button (index 204).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('abhishekedla9133@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('8309176874')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Doctors' link in the main navigation to open the Doctors page and then wait for the page to load so the search field becomes available.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type 'Dr' into the search field (index 1463), submit the search (press Enter), then open the first visible doctor card (click 'View Profile & Book' button at index 1571) to view profile and availability.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[2]/div/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Dr')
        
        # -> Click the first doctor's 'View Profile & Book' button (index 1984) to open the profile and then verify the 'Availability' text is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[3]/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Assert URL contains '/'
        assert "/" in frame.url
        
        # Verify Doctor result card (Book Appointment button) is visible
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/section[2]/div/div/div[1]/div/button').nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert await elem.is_visible()
        
        # Verify 'Availability' text is visible — element not present in available elements, report issue
        raise AssertionError("Element with text 'Availability' not found in the available elements list; cannot verify availability visibility.")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Search with unknown term shows No results found message

        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (click if link exists on page; otherwise use direct navigation).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Dismiss the Data Privacy & Security Consent by clicking 'Accept & Continue', then fill the email and password fields and click 'Sign In'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('abhishekedla9133@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('8309176874')
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button to submit the login form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button (element index 265) to submit the login form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url
        assert '/hospitals' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Hospital results list').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC010-View hospital details from a hospital card
description: Verifies clicking a hospital card shows details including services and contact information.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (explicit navigation step as specified in test).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url
        assert '/hospitals' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"hospital-results") or contains(@id,"hospital-results") or @aria-label="Hospital results list"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//section[contains(@class,"hospital-details") or @aria-label="Hospital details" or contains(@id,"hospital-details")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Services').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Contact').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:5173/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Accept the Data Privacy & Security Consent, fill the email and password fields with the provided credentials, and click 'Sign In' to attempt login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the current Sign In button (element index 265) to submit the login form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the email and password using the current input elements ([259] and [260]) and click the current Sign In button ([265]) to attempt login. Then wait for the page to change.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('abhishekedla9133@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('8309176874')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill email into input [364], fill password into input [365], then click Sign In button [370] to attempt login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('abhishekedla9133@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('8309176874')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Abhishek Edla' profile link (element index 1140) to open the Profile page, then wait for the page to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url
        assert '/profile' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Profile updated').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

title: TC030-Show validation errors when contact fields have invalid values
description: Verifies the Profile form blocks save and shows field-level validation messages for invalid contact inputs.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Accept cookie consent, fill the login form with the provided credentials, and click Sign In to log into the app.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('abhishekedla9133@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('8309176874')
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button to submit the login form (index 82).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button (now available as index 154) to submit the login form and proceed to the dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the login form with test credentials and click the Sign In button, then wait for navigation/rendering.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('abhishekedla9133@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('8309176874')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Open the user menu (Abhishek Edla) to access the Profile link and then open the Profile page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/header/div/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url
        assert '/profile' in frame.url
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/main/section[2]/div/div[2]/div[1]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        assert await elem.is_visible()
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/main/section[2]/div/div[2]/div[1]/form/button').nth(0)
        assert await elem.is_visible()
        raise AssertionError('Email and/or phone fields or their validation error messages are not present on the profile page; cannot verify field-level validation for invalid contact inputs.')
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
